name: Node
observations: Manages node configurations and connections
Mentioned as a core component in README.md

The Node class now stores tokens as a dictionary of lists (Dict[str, List[NodeToken]]) to handle multiple tokens with the same ID but different types. This change was implemented to correctly handle cases where FBC and RPC tokens share the same ID, which is common in the system.

The add_token method in the Node class has been updated to store tokens in lists grouped by token ID, check if a token of the same type already exists for a given ID, replace existing tokens of the same type rather than adding duplicates, and allow multiple tokens with the same ID but different types to coexist.

These changes are validated by the test_same_token_id_different_types_command_execution test case in tests/commander/test_command_execution.py which ensures proper differentiation and handling of FBC and RPC tokens with identical IDs.

name: Root Cause Analysis
observations: The NodeManager loads configuration from nodes_test.json by default, which only contains 1 token (163 RPC) for AP01m
The nodes.json file has the correct configuration with tokens 162 FBC, 163 VNC, and 164 RPC for AP01m
When scan_log_files processes the FBC files, it only finds a match for 162_FBC.log because:

Documentation updated with 'Saving Configuration' section and 'v1.6.0' in Version History.
Overall task completion verified.

name: Telnet Prompt Detection
observations: Require newline anchors in prompt regex patterns
Support multiple common prompt formats
Always test with outputs containing percentage symbols

name: TelnetSession
observations: Class for managing Telnet sessions in the SessionManager
Applied same prompt pattern fix as TelnetClient to prevent command output truncation
Replaced single broad regex pattern with multiple specific patterns with proper anchoring

name: Telnet Output Truncation Fix
observations: Root Cause: Overly broad regex pattern `\d+[a-z]\%\s*$` in `TelnetClient._read_response` caused premature truncation of command output.
Solution: Implemented multiple, more robust regex patterns with newline anchors in `TelnetClient` and `TelnetSession` for accurate prompt detection.
Verification: All integration tests in `tests/commander/test_telnet_command_output.py` passed, confirming full and consistent output from both context menu and Telnet tab executions.
Learnings: Importance of precise regex for protocol parsing, dynamic test creation to unblock workflows, iterative debugging of tests.
Documentation updated in `docs/user/telnet_guide.md` to include details of the fix, root cause, solution, and verification.

name: TelnetClient.py
observations: Modified to include `prompt_patterns` list and updated `_read_response` method for multi-pattern detection.

name: TelnetSession (in session_manager.py)
observations: Modified to include `prompt_patterns` list and updated `_read_response` method for multi-pattern detection, mirroring changes in `TelnetClient`.

name: test_telnet_command_output.py
observations: New integration tests created to simulate Telnet connections, execute commands from context menu and Telnet tab, and compare outputs.
Tests debugged to fix signal name mismatches, presenter mocking, and duplicate signal connections.

name: CommandWorker Error Handling Fix
observations: Modified the CommandWorker's error handling logic in src/commander/command_queue.py to preserve full .rpc command output while still correctly indicating command success/failure. Removed the line that was truncating self.result to 200 characters when an error was detected, ensuring that the full response is always available while maintaining proper success/failure status reporting.

name: RPC Command Logging Test
observations: Test `tests/commander/test_rpc_log_path.py` executed successfully.
The test passed with 1 warning related to `telnetlib` deprecation.
The warning is not critical to the RPC command logging functionality.

name: RpcCommandService.get_token_log_path_fix
observations: Modified `RpcCommandService.get_token` to populate `log_path` in `NodeToken`.
Used `NodeManager._generate_log_path` for path generation.

name: test_rpc_log_path.py
observations: New test file created to verify RPC command output logging.
Test simulates RPC command execution and verifies log file content.

name: RPC Command Output Logging
observations: Ensures RPC command output is written to designated log files.
Documentation updated in docs/architecture/command_processing_architecture.md to reflect the fix for RPC command output logging.
The fix involved modifying RpcCommandService.get_token to use NodeManager._generate_log_path.
Verification was done through tests/commander/test_rpc_log_path.py.
